codeReview.summary: QualityPreset component implementation is correct and fully covers the feature requirements. Bounds enforcement uses Math.min(Math.max(value, range.min), range.max) on both slider change and mouse up events. CRF ranges match specification: Compact 28-35, Good 22-28, High 18-23, Custom 18-35. Preset descriptions visible, audio bitrate display per preset works. Tests in QualityPreset.test.tsx are comprehensive (36 test cases covering rendering, preset selection, slider bounds, keyboard accessibility, audio display).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff reported 'ESLint not installed - lint command fails' but ESLint IS installed and npm run lint works (only warnings, no errors). This misinformation could mislead future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[1]: 'ESLint not installed - lint command fails'. Verified: node_modules/eslint exists, npm run lint exits 0 with 15 warnings only.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill instructs to verify using agent-browser, but the skill does not document that Electron app verification requires ffmpeg binary to be installed. Worker spent time attempting agent-browser verification before discovering the blocker.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows agent-browser opened http://localhost:5173, then handoff notes: 'Cannot test actual IPC communication without backend' and 'agent-browser verification requires Electron app with ffmpeg binary'. Skill procedure doesn't mention ffmpeg prerequisite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When preset is selected, handlePresetClick triggers 3 separate IPC calls: updateItemSettings(preset), updateItemSettings(crf), updateItemSettings(audioBitrate). Each callback also fetches queue state separately. This creates redundant IPC traffic but is not a functional bug.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: QualityPreset.tsx:handlePresetClick calls onPresetChange, onCrfChange, onAudioBitrateChange in sequence. App.tsx handlePresetChange calls updateItemSettings({preset}) then getQueueState; handleCrfChange calls updateItemSettings({crf}) then getQueueState; handleAudioBitrateChange calls updateItemSettings({audioBitrate}) then getQueueState. Total: 3x updateItemSettings + 3x getQueueState per preset change.

summary: QualityPreset component implementation passes review. All feature requirements met: 4 preset buttons with correct CRF bounds (Compact 28-35, Good 22-28, High 18-23, Custom 18-35), slider snaps to boundaries via Math.min(Math.max()) on change and mouseUp events, audio bitrate display per preset, preset descriptions visible. Comprehensive unit tests (36 cases) cover all functionality. Three shared state observations documented: ESLint misinformation in handoff, missing ffmpeg prerequisite in skill docs, and non-blocking redundant IPC calls on preset change.
